questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A is three times as old as B, and the sum of their ages is $48$ years. A's age is _____ years.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Express both ages in one variable.

**Working.** $A=3B$ and $A+B=48\Rightarrow 4B=48\Rightarrow B=12,\;A=36$.

**Answer.** $36$ years.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The square root of $196$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Find the number whose square is $196$.

**Working.** $14^2 = 196$.

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the best connective: '____ the equipment was old, it functioned reliably.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A contrast between two clauses is introduced by a concessive conjunction.

**Working.** Old equipment working reliably is unexpected, so a contrast word ('Although') is needed; 'Because' would imply causation.

**Answer.** Although.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of $10, 20, 30, 40$ and $50$ is _____ .

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A can finish a job in $12$ days and B in $6$ days. Working together, they finish it in _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add the per-day work rates.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{12}+\dfrac{1}{6}=\dfrac{1}{12}+\dfrac{2}{12}=\dfrac{3}{12}=\dfrac{1}{4}$, so $4$ days.

**Answer.** $4$ days.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Arrange the sentences into a coherent paragraph.
P: As a result, the roof was reinforced with additional bolts.
Q: During inspection, engineers noticed hairline cracks in the roof.
R: They immediately evacuated the section as a precaution.
S: A detailed survey then confirmed that the rock was unstable.
The correct order is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Arrange by cause-and-effect and chronological cues.

**Working.** Cracks are noticed (Q), the area is evacuated as a precaution (R), a survey confirms instability (S), and as a result bolts are added (P).

**Answer.** Q, R, S, P.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The measure of each interior angle of a regular hexagon is _____ degrees.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: By selling $20$ articles, a trader gains the cost price of $5$ articles. The profit percentage is _____ % .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit\% = (gain in cost units) / (cost units sold) $\times 100$.

**Working.** Gain $=5\,\text{CP}$ on a cost of $20\,\text{CP}$, so $\dfrac{5}{20}\times 100 = 25\%$.

**Answer.** $25\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The area of a circle of radius $7$ is _____ (use $\pi=22/7$).

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: On a standard die, opposite faces sum to $7$. If the top face shows $2$, the bottom face shows

figure.caption: A die with two pips showing on its top face.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='25' y='25' width='70' height='70' rx='8' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.08)' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='45' cy='45' r='6' fill='#334155'/><circle cx='75' cy='75' r='6' fill='#334155'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Opposite faces of a standard die add to $7$.

**Working.** $7-2=5$.

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: Applied Geology · Remote Sensing & GIS
questions.stem: Linear features on satellite imagery that often reflect faults or fractures are called
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Lineaments are mappable linear features expressing structural control.

**Working.**
Lineaments.

**Answer.** Lineaments

questions.subject: Economic & Ore Geology
questions.stem: Bauxite, the ore of aluminium, forms primarily by
options: intense lateritic weathering in the tropics
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Bauxite is a residual lateritic weathering product (tropical/humid).

**Working.**
Lateritic weathering.

**Answer.** Lateritic weathering

questions.subject: Economic & Ore Geology
questions.stem: Chromite and platinum-group deposits typically form by
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Chromite/PGE concentrate by magmatic (cumulate) segregation in mafic–ultramafic layered intrusions.

**Working.**
Magmatic segregation.

**Answer.** Magmatic segregation

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A coal seam dips $40^\circ$ true. A mine drift trends at $35^\circ$ to the strike. The apparent dip in the drift is _____ degrees.

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $30^\circ$ and dips $55^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=55^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $55^\circ$

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: On an equal-area (Schmidt) stereonet, a plane is represented as a

figure.caption: A dipping plane as a great circle

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A plane plots as a great circle; its pole plots as a point.

**Working.**
Great circle.

**Answer.** Great circle
